title: Pharaoh + Cleopatra

cpu: AMD A6-6400K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes:
This is an update to my last review.

Following the release of Proton 4.11-1, Pharaoh will often not get past the opening cutscene unless either d3d11 or d3d9 are disabled in launch options. Even when it does get past the cutscene, the display is moved down about a fifth of the screen (cutting a fifth of the display off and leaving the top fifth black) but the inputs are not, meaning you will always click a fifth of the screen below your cursor. This obviously makes the game unplayable. Disabling esync and fsync have no effect, and reverting to Proton 4.2-9 fixes this bug.

Using the widescreen fix has no effect, other than fixing the scrolling bug mentioned before (which is obviously useless since it's unplayable anyway). Another effect of this bug is that the menus cannot be accessed in-game.

I currently have found no fix for this. For the time being, run this game through 4.2-9 using the widescreen fix, and it will run perfectly.
title: Pharaoh + Cleopatra

cpu: AMD E2-6110 APU with AMD Radeon R2 Graphics
notes: The game runs flawlessly as-is out of the box on Proton 4.11-7; no issues with scrolling or anything. However I haven't had any luck getting the widescreen fix to work - it causes the title bar to turn black and clicking any resolution in the options menu causes the entire screen to go black and not come back (but audio keeps playing), necessitating ending the process.
title: Pharaoh + Cleopatra

notes: works perfect out of the box, decrease scroll speed and use the resolution fix mentioned in the other comments
title: Pharaoh + Cleopatra
